title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Execution Trace: Feed Go Progressive Rollout
summary: The image runs as root, startup mutates schema on every replica, readiness ignores PostgreSQL and backlog, shutdown drops leases, and rollout lacks version compatibility. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go progressive rollout increment in deployable Go feed-service release unit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.
Run migrations through one owned release step with locks, timeout, compatibility gates, evidence, and explicit failure handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Progressive Rollout · Theory Feed Go Progressive Rollout
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2937 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-progressive-rollout-yumlui(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_progressive_rollout_yumlui\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?artifacts < 3)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?inspected != artifacts)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?healthStates < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rolloutFailures > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Feed Go Progressive Rollout to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Feed Go Progressive Rollout.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
